sections.paragraph:
imp. אֱסֹף, אֶסְפָה Num 11:16 pl. אִסְפוּ Psa 50:5 fut. יֶאֱסֹף; in pl. and with suff. יַאַסְפוּ, יַאַסְפֵנִי; more rarely with א quiescent, or cast away יֹסֵף, וַיֹּסֶף, אֹסֵף 1Sa 15:6 2Sa 6:1 Psa 104:29, prop. to scrape, to scrape together, zufammenfcharren, cogn. to the verbs סוּף (whence סוּפָה sweeping whirlwind), סָפָה; also יָסַף, and with the harsher חָסֵף (which see); hence-

(1) to collect, as fruits, Exo 23:10 ears of corn, Rth 2:7 money, 2Ki 22:4 also to assemble men, a people, peoples, etc., Exo 3:16 Num 21:16 2Sa 12:28. Const. with an acc., also sometimes with אֶל of the person or place to which any one is congregated; Gen 42:17, וַיֶּאֱסֹף אֹתָם אֶל־מִשְׁמָר “and he assembled them into the prison;” 1Sa 14:52 2Sa 11:27 Gen 6:21 also עַל 2Ki 22:20. Hence-

(2) to take to oneself, to receive to oneself, especially to hospitality and protection, Deu 22:2 Jos 20:4 אָסַף פ׳ מִצָּרַעַת 2Ki 5:3, seq. “to receive any one from leprosy,” i.e. to heal a leprous person, after which he would be again received into intercourse with other men.

(3) to gather up to one, to contract, draw back; Gen 49:33, “he drew up his feet into the bed;” 1Sa 14:19, אֱסֹף יָדֶךָ “draw back thy hand,” i.e. stay from what thou hast begun; Joe 2:10, “the stars אָֽסְפוּ נָגְהָם shall draw back their brightness,” i.e. they shall shine no longer. Hence-

(4) to take away, especially that which any one had previously given; Psa 104:29, תֹּסֵף רוּחָם יִגְוָעוּן “thou takest away their breath, they expire;” Job 34:14 Gen 30:23, אָסַף אֱלֹהִים אֶת־חֶרְפָּתִי “God hath taken away my shame,” Isa 4:1 10:14 .

(5) to take out of the way, to destroy, to kill, Jud. 18:25 1Sa 15:6, פֶּן אֹסִפְךָ עִמֹּו “lest I destroy you with them;” Eze 34:29, אֲסוּפֵי רָעָב “destroyed,” i.e. killed “by hunger;” Jer 8:13 Zep 1:2 compare the roots סָפָה, סוּף. From the first signification comes-

(6) agmen claudere, to bring up the rear, Isa 58:8. Those who bring up the rear, keep together, and collect the stragglers; compare Piel, No. 3.

Niphal-

(1) to be collected, gathered together; construed with אֶל Lev 26:25 עַל 2Sa 17:11 לְ 2Ch 30:3 of the place to which any are gathered together, although עַל in this phrase more frequently signifies against, Gen 34:30 Psa 35:15.-נֶאֱסַף אֶל־עַמֹּו Gen 49:29 אֶל־אֲבֹותָיו Jud. 2:10 , and simply נֶאֱסַף Num 20:26, “to be gathered to one’s people, to one’s father;” used of entering into Hades, where the Hebrews regarded their ancestors as being gathered together. This gathering to one’s fathers, or one’s people, is distinguished both from death and burial, Gen 25:8 35:29 2Ki 22:20. The principle is different of the passages in which נֶאֱסַף is used of gathering the dead bodies of those slain in battle for burial, Jer 8:2 Eze 29:5 Job 27:19.

(2) to be received (compare Kal No. 2); used of the leper, i.q. to be healed, Num 12:14 Jer 47:6 reflect. to receive, or betake oneself (into the sheath), of a sword.

(3) to be taken away, to vanish, to perish, Isa 16:10 60:20 Jer 48:33 Hos 4:3.

Piel-

(1) i.q. Kal No. 1, to gather, collect, Isa 62:9.

(2) to receive, as a guest, Jud. 19:18 .

(3) i.q. Kal No. 6, to bring up the rear, Num 10:25 Jos 6:9, 13 Jos 6:13 Isa 52:12.

Pual pass. of Kal No. 1, to be gathered, Isa 24:22 33:4 .

Hithpael, to be assembled, Deu 33:5.

The derivatives follow, also אָסִיף.
